description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

Deliverable:
End-to-end integration tests for the five scenarios in the spec. Use test/test_support/state_json_fixture.ex (S0) to control state.json contents across scenario runs. Each scenario has cardinality + tier-set assertions.

Scenarios:
1. Hard-coupled MFA, drift fires on both tiers (specled.realized_by.scenario.implication_drift_both_tiers).
2. Existing duplication validator-warns and is cleanable (specled.realized_by.scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns) — exercises validator alongside dedup task; coordinate with S7+S8.
3. Bare-module first-run silent seed, then drift on change (specled.realized_by.scenario.bare_module_first_run_silent_seed + bare_module_drift_after_seed).
4. Adding public export to bare-module-tracked module fires drift on both tiers (specled.realized_by.scenario.bare_module_export_added).
5. Dangling MFA reports once tier=implementation (specled.realized_by.scenario.dangling_implication_once).

Plus: bare-module not-loadable dangles (specled.realized_by.scenario.bare_module_not_loadable_dangles); inferred flag does not leak (specled.realized_by.scenario.inferred_flag_does_not_leak).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: new integration test file (test/specled_ex/realization/implication_integration_test.exs or similar) green for all five scenarios + the two extras.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: each scenario's specled.realized_by.scenario.* id resolves and the test asserts the spec's then-clause.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/** (no production code changes — scenarios test what S1-S5 already shipped)
- .spec/** (specs are advanced by S9, not here)
Allowed touches:
- test/specled_ex/realization/implication_integration_test.exs (new) OR appended scenarios in existing integration files
- test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs (only if a scenario is more naturally a unit-orchestrator test)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT add new production code in this stage.
- Do NOT modify the validator (S7) or dedup task (S8); scenario 2 only depends on them being landed first.
- Use StateJsonFixture (S0) for state.json control. Never run integration scenarios against the real .spec/state.json.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

- scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns — validator + drift coexistence
- scenario.bare_module_first_run_silent_seed + bare_module_drift_after_seed — seed then drift
- scenario.bare_module_export_added — public function added fires both tiers
- scenario.dangling_implication_once — exactly one dangling, tier=implementation
- scenario.bare_module_not_loadable_dangles — unloadable bare module path

  `execute: false` in .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md. Per the ticket's

  spec-verification flips and dup-cover cleanup land in 701.10.)

Mechanical scope check:

Summary: Added one integration test file with seven describe blocks, one
per spec scenario id. Each test drives Orchestrator.run/2 against
compile-on-disk fixture modules and a per-test tmp <root>/.spec/state.json
seeded via StateJsonFixture. Scenario 1 (hard-coupled MFA) seeds both
tiers directly via Implementation.hashes_for_seeding/3 + StateJsonFixture
to avoid the post-run HashStore.write/2 path clobbering impl-tier seeds
(a known orchestrator nuance documented in
lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex moduledoc — out of scope for
this ticket per "no production code changes"). Bare-module scenarios
exercise the silent-seed merge path. The compile_module! helper purges
before and after each recompile and forces debug_info on so
:code.get_object_code/1 reads the new BEAM rather than the previously
loaded copy.

VERIFY: PASS (orchestrator-relayed from verifier session a6e06cd62067e8ac7 — the verifier's first bw command landed truncated as "-m"; this comment relays its findings verbatim from the agent return)
Session: a6e06cd62067e8ac7 (subagent of eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991/subagents/agent-a6e06cd62067e8ac7.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 1 (test/specled_ex/realization/implication_integration_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
- No lib/** or .spec/** edits.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0, passed: 0 (`.spec/**` Out of Scope; spec-verification flips + dup-cover cleanup explicitly land in S9/701.10)
- Requirements with passing tests: 7/7 scenarios — all 5 ticket scenarios + 2 extras present, each as a describe with @tag spec listing the scenario id and covered requirements. Cardinality (length == 1 / == 0) and tier-set (api_boundary/implementation) assertions present in every scenario. StateJsonFixture (S0) used throughout; real .spec/state.json never touched. Cross-cutting refute_inferred_leak/1 invoked per-scenario plus a dedicated inferred_flag_does_not_leak test.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none mechanical. See Notes.
- Project verification: pass — `mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implication_integration_test.exs` 7/7 green; full `mix test` 768/0. `mix spec.check` residuals at base ref ff25db3 are pre-existing (3× overlap/duplicate_covers from S1-S5 scenario authoring + branch_guard_missing_subject_update for the new test file because `.spec/**` is Out of Scope for this ticket — both belong to S9/701.10).

Verdict: PASS

Notes for the auditor (workarounds flagged for scrutiny):
(a) Scenario 1 (hard-coupled MFA drift) seeds BOTH tiers directly via Implementation.hashes_for_seeding/3 + StateJsonFixture.seed/2 to dodge a HashStore.write/2 clobber path that the implementer claims is documented in the orchestrator moduledoc. Implementer chose this seeding workaround over a production fix per "no production code changes". Auditor should confirm the workaround does not bypass the actual implication-drift-both-tiers behavior the spec scenario is meant to demonstrate.
(b) Scenario 3's body-only assertion presumes hash_module_head_union/2 is body-insensitive for the chosen fixture (`def alpha, do: :alpha_changed`). Mutation: changing only the body should NOT fire api_boundary drift but SHOULD fire implementation drift. Auditor should confirm the fixture genuinely exercises this — not pass for orthogonal reasons.
(c) Scenario 2 stitches the validator + orchestrator together via two separate calls plus a deliberately-wrong-hash HashStore.write/2 — an acceptable proxy that does NOT exercise both in one `mix spec.check` invocation. Auditor should confirm this is fit-for-purpose for the spec scenario specled.realized_by.scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns or whether it's a meaningful gap.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: ab6a7bedd0c2947fe
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991/subagents/agent-ab6a7bedd0c2947fe.jsonl

Reasoning:
(a) Scenario 1's dual-tier StateJsonFixture seed is justified. The clobber path is documented at lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex:24-33 (moduledoc: "implementation tier is excluded from hash commit ... follow-up work is expected to expose an implementation hash-refresh helper") plus lib/specled_ex/realization/hash_store.ex:81-93 (write/2 has replacement semantics). refresh_and_commit_hashes/3 (orchestrator.ex:536-555) only iterates @flat_tiers and then calls HashStore.write/2, which atomically replaces the realization map and would erase any silent-seed impl entry on a no-drift run. The test still demonstrates the spec scenario's then-clause: implication_integration_test.exs:190-191 asserts length(api_drift) == 1 AND length(impl_drift) == 1 on head mutation; lines 239-243 assert impl drift fires AND no api_boundary drift on body-only mutation. Cardinality + tier-set both rigorous.

(b) hash_module_head_union/2 is genuinely body-insensitive on this fixture. canonical.ex:263-274 head_ast_for_export/4 maps clauses to {normalize(args), normalize(guards)} - body is structurally absent from the envelope. The fixture mutates `def alpha, do: :a` -> `def alpha, do: :alpha_changed` (same single clause, zero args, no guards, only body literal differs), so api_boundary hash is provably invariant. Test (implication_integration_test.exs:417-438) asserts length(impl_drift) == 1 AND api_drift == [] - pass cannot be for orthogonal reasons.

(c) Scenario 2's split-call proxy is fit-for-purpose. The spec scenario specled.realized_by.scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns (.spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md:337-347) has three then-clauses: (1) one redundant_dup warning, (2) names MFA + points to dedup task, (3) drift behavior unchanged because implication still tracks under api_boundary. The test asserts all three at implication_integration_test.exs:282-289 (cardinality + MFA-form text + dedup-task pointer) and lines 296-322 (one api_boundary drift after wrong-hash committed via HashStore.write/2 - proves the implication-amplified entry coexists with the explicit listing without double-firing). The spec scenario does not require an end-to-end mix spec.check roundtrip nor an after-cleanup re-run; the verifier's flag was over-cautious.

Mechanical: 1 file touched (test/specled_ex/realization/implication_integration_test.exs), 0 outside Allowed globs, 0 in Out-of-Scope globs, 7 describes / 7 @tag spec / 7/7 green via mix test on the file.

title: S6: Integration scenarios 1-5
